message:
refactor(sync): decompose mutex lock future into testable state-machine helpers

The monolithic LockFuture::poll() method contained interleaved waiter
management, poison checking, and lock acquisition logic that was difficult
to reason about and had subtle ordering dependencies.

Extract focused helper functions:
- remove_waiter(): removes a waiter by ID from any position in the queue
- waiter_has_predecessor(): checks if a waiter is blocked behind another
- update_waiter_waker(): updates a registered waiter's waker if changed
- register_waiter(): allocates a new waiter ID and appends to the queue
- take_waiter_and_next_waker(): cleanup helper for cancellation/drop paths
- poll_lock_state(): single state-transition function returning a LockPollState
  enum (Ready, Pending, Poisoned) that encapsulates all lock acquisition logic

LockFuture::poll() now delegates to poll_lock_state() and only handles
the outer cancellation check and response mapping, reducing its cyclomatic
complexity from ~12 to ~4.

The waiter removal logic is also fixed: previously, removing a non-front
waiter could leave the mutex in a state where the front waiter was never
woken. Now remove_waiter_and_take_next_waker() correctly distinguishes
front vs non-front removal and only propagates the wake chain when the
front waiter was removed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
